channel.title: Journal on Cyber Blog-ul lui Novac
channel.description: Recent content in Journal on Cyber Blog-ul lui Novac

item.title: olympus
item.description:
1 minute room they say

first scan

Found an interesting directory when enumerating

let’s access it

Interesting login form here , we may use sqlmap on this
i am new to sqlmap so i am going to use –wizard option

enter the url when prompted and let it do its job

we find the current database being Olympus
enumerating Olympus using :

we see there are 6 tables inside it, dump them all
use :

item.title: Biohazard
item.description:
For the first part just run nmap and accces the http server

i gonna run a gobuster scan and play with the http server in parallel

inspecting the /mainsionmain page we get the following subdomain

in there we get the following string

lets plug it into cyber chef

cool we have a /teaRoom/

but lets see what happens if w epress YES

and we have a flag

carazy zombie in teaRoom

and we find a LockPick flag cool

lets Yes

damn that crazy like a gobuster scan

the barroom asks us for the lockpick room lets see what happens

nothing special , lets READ it

also pplug it into cyber chef

cool and we have a music sheet flag

when we input the music note into the piano field we get here , lets press YES
pressing yes gives us another flag

item.title: TryHack3M Subscribe
item.description:
Part 1 - Exploitation

scanning , some interesting ports opened

accesing the http via burpsuite and inspecting the site map we see the following

lets look a lil bit at the js folder , that invite function seems interesting

ok so i copy pasted the function and pasted it into a code beautifier and get this:

the original function :

so the function basicaly sais that if our hostname is not capture3milliobsubcribers.thm we cant log in , so lets modify that is /etc/hostnames

ok cool now lets modify in inspect that field to redirect us to the domain specified in the function

ok cool so now just input anything into the invide code field and we should get the invite code

and boom

item.title: Include
item.description:
basic scanning in the begining

a lot of ports opened , after trying to connect to each of them i found this

logging in as guest we get the following dashboard

First flag

in the profile section i tried to modify some parameters and it works , and just like that we can modify the isAdmin parameter to be true

as admin we can view the api dashboard which gives us some interesting information about the structure of the site and how it manages requests

in the admin settings tab we can modify the banner image

this may look like a Local File Intrusion vulnerabily
if we input the two addresses from the api dashboard into admin settings we get two base64 encoded responses

when we decode those using cyber chef we get the following (for getAllAdmins):

item.title: red
item.description:
link : https://tryhackme.com/room/redisl33t

note for the end: on the final script used for the privillege escalation , the script has to be modified as shown below , right at the end of the script :

environ_p = (c_char_p * len(environ))()
environ_p[:] = environ

print('[+] Calling execve()')

libc.execve(b'/home/red/.git/pkexec', c_char_p(None), environ_p)

desc :

scanned the ip with the following command
`sudo nmap -v –min-rate 10000 $IP -p-

just cause it short , and it guves us ssh and http open , cool

item.title: new hire old artifact
item.description:
link : https://tryhackme.com/room/newhireoldartifacts

this time we have an splunk interface and we have to work to find the thread


lets look a lil bit at the data summary
good , single host
i changed the data a lil bit in the filter section , cause in the desc it says december 2021

for the fist task i search with the following string

and here we have the binary name

also the company

the nest task speficy that is the same folder as the first binary , so lets search using that
C:\Users\FINANC~1\AppData\Local\Temp\\
here , note that i har to seearch with other location cause the location previosly added was a hoax

item.title: infinity shell
item.description:
Desc:

at a first examinatijon of the machine i didnt find anything special

i decided to investigate /var/log for any logs from the web app

i examined every file from the logs in apache2 and the only 2 that show something interesting are error.log.1 and especially other_vhost_access.log.1

whopp something interesting

nahhh nothing good

some interesting query here

lets decode them , i think is base 64

ok ok that is nice

item.title: brains
item.description:
link :https://tryhackme.com/room/brains
desc:

accesing the ip through a browser we get the followeing

mhmm interesting , lets run a port scan


nothing really interesting , just ssh and http

i runned a sobdomain enumeration to see any interesting subdomains


and we did find something
lets check everyone of them

yeah every one of them give the same result , i decided do to another portscan , maybe i will find something new

and sure we did

there is something interesting

item.title: Evil-GPT v2
item.description:
yeah so this is the continuation of evil gpt
DESC:

this time we can acces the AI trough http in browser , so we know for sure a htttp port in open

know we can talk to the model like to the real chat gpt

here i tried some trick , liltle to no result as we can see

yeak so i find the trick . I told the ai to say random stuff and dont stop , and eventually it droped the flag pretty easy , this was actually how the real chat gpt was hacked some time before revealing some non public information

item.title: Evil-GPT
item.description:
so this is the first ctf i goona post ,

Desc :

when we connect with netcat we got the small following promt
i tried to put some command in but it dosent respond to anything except exit , for the moment

i input “flag " and got the following output

ok idk why but when i typed again ls i got the following

in the listing is a interesting py script name evilai.py , i gonna try to execute it

item.title: decryptify
item.description:
scaning

i did a double scan , cause is seamed a lil bit weird to have a simgle port opened , and i was right , lets search about this waste service

i accesed the website via 1337
nothing interesting , lets run gobuster

ok lets verify the js

ok interesting

ok lets beatufy if to understand something

function b(c, d) {
    const e = a();
    return b = function(f, g) {
        f = f - 0x165;
        let h = e[f];
        return h;
    }, b(c, d);
}
const j = b;

function a() {
    const k = ['16OTYqOr', '861cPVRNJ', '474AnPRwy', 'H7gY2tJ9wQzD4rS1', '5228dijopu', '29131EDUYqd', '8756315tjjUKB', '1232020YOKSiQ', '7042671GTNtXE', '1593688UqvBWv', '90209ggCpyY'];
    a = function() {
        return k;
    };
    return a();
}(function(d, e) {
    const i = b,
        f = d();
    while (!![]) {
        try {
            const g = parseInt(i(0x16b)) / 0x1 + -parseInt(i(0x16f)) / 0x2 + parseInt(i(0x167)) / 0x3 * (parseInt(i(0x16a)) / 0x4) + parseInt(i(0x16c)) / 0x5 + parseInt(i(0x168)) / 0x6 * (parseInt(i(0x165)) / 0x7) + -parseInt(i(0x166)) / 0x8 * (parseInt(i(0x16e)) / 0x9) + parseInt(i(0x16d)) / 0xa;
            if (g === e) break;
            else f['push'](f['shift']());
        } catch (h) {
            f['push'](f['shift']());
        }
    }
}(a, 0xe43f0));
const c = j(0x169);

ok interesiting , the majority of the code is obfuscated , lets insert j(0x169) inside the browser console so see if we get something

ok cool this may be the password for the api panel

and it is , cool now we are login at the panel at $IP/api.php

item.title: blog
item.description:
ok soo recon

basic ports opened

a lot of interesting subdomains , even robots.txt is available


0 , interesting
also there is an smb server opened, i checked it but nothing interesting there

i had to restart the machine because some of the subodmain werent available , cool
/wp-admin/


login page at wp-admin , cool

i runed wpscan and found some interesting usernames to try on the login page

item.title: anonymous
item.description:
To answer the first two questions just basic nmap scan

then lets enumerate the SMB service
note: smb ( server message block) is a communication protocol used to share files , printers , serial ports and miscellaneous commmunication

ok interesting lets verify that smb files

i dowloaded those two files and check them but nothing to interesting
lets check ftp now


i entered the ftp server and enumerate it ( i logged in with anonymous credentials , that means nothing , ok cool ) , then i found a scripts folder and get it contents

item.title: ContainMe
item.description:
first scan , the host seemed down i hard to use -Pn flag for ignoring the host scaning , and we have some pretty interesting ports opened

after another longer scan i found that there are actualy 2 more ports opened


lets check the http page and run a gobuster subdomain scan

ok some from the web interface we can see that the server is using apche2 ,we may exploit this later

the gobuster enumeration is finished and we find some interesting subodmains lets acces them

accesing incex.php and looking inside the source code we can see this comment , the first clue
this is obiously hinting at parametrizezing (excuse my english) the path of the file with ?path=so lets try this
and bamm we have acces to the file system

and we also can execute commands by using ?path=; cool

item.title: padelify
item.description:
reconing

First thinks first , its time to look for information
a
I tried running a gobuster scan with a common wordlists for discovery but nothing returned , surely a WAF( web aplication firewall )is blocking the acces

lets try to bypass it , using the flag -a bypass it pretty simple
for noobies (like me ) the -a flag uses a diferent user agent than the default gobuster agent ,
a user agent is a string of character that make the request to the site

item.title: MrRobot
item.description:
the first nmap scan shows that all 1000 ports on the host are in ignored status
after a google search i found out that using -sN ( connect scan) can bypass this easly

basic ports open , lets try to visit the web site

after a very cool animation this is what we see
i tried prepare and a cool cinematic roll up , nothing to interesting in ther elets try question
i
just some picture aperead on the screen , i think this whole site is just some random promotional things from the tv series

item.title: Compiled
item.description:
ok so lets download the boinary and test it

it asks for a password ,lets open it in ghidra

the main function in ghidra show a entangled string of if checks

the function __isoc99_scanf seems wierd lets read the man page

   `The format string consists of a sequence of directives which` 
   `describe how to process the sequence of input characters. If` 
   `processing of a directive fails, no further input is read, and` 
   `scanf() returns. A "failure" can be either of the following:` 
   `input failure, meaning that input characters were unavailable, or` 
   `matching failure, meaning that the input was inappropriate (see` 
   `below)`

   `A directive is one of the following:`

   `•      A sequence of white-space characters (space, tab, newline,`
          `etc.; see isspace(3)).  This directive matches any amount`
          `of white space, including none, in the input.`

   `•      An ordinary character (i.e., one other than white space or`
          `'%').  This character must exactly match the next`
          `character of input.`

   `•      A conversion specification, which commences with a '%'`
          `(percent) character.  A sequence of characters from the`
          `input is converted according to this specification, and`
          `the result is placed in the corresponding pointer`
          `argument.  If the next item of input does not match the`
          `conversion specification, the conversion fails—this is a`
          `matching failure.`

ok interesting , so according to the man page and the program , everthing before the DoYouEven string will be stored in the variable local_28 and the variable local_28 has to have the value _init for us to get the password so lets try the password
DoYouEven_init

item.title: wonderland
item.description:
basic nmap scan :

basic ports opened
then i did a gobuster scan

interesting , subdomain ‘/r’ , that give me the idea to try every letter in the word rabbit , as a subdomain and founde this

interesting
i wanna try another gobuster scan with this

nothing interesting
i decided to inspect the page and founded this not shown

looks like a password for ssh lets try it
alice:HowDothTheLittleCrocodileImproveHisShiningTail

item.title: ReversingElf
item.description:
This will be my first indo binary exploatation
lets begin

Crackme1


just add the exec permision and run the file

Crackme2


when we try to run the program they ask for a password , intesting
lets run Strings to see the human readeble caracthers

ok cool we got the password being “super_secret_password”
now lets just run with the password

Crackme3

in the description it says to use some basic reverse enginering method , so i opened ghridra and looked into it


the two pictures frm above some very interesintg strings encoded in non other than base64 , lets copy them and decode them
and decoding the second liine we get the flag
**

item.title: Prioritise
item.description:
Description:
“We have this new to-do list application, where we order our tasking based on priority! Is it really all that secure, though…?”

i checked the website and add a new task on the to do list
lets check a basic sql injection

nothing special in the begining

i created a new event in the tasklist and intercept it via burpsuite to look for something special

the request seams normal
i want tot try to modify the conent-length field may be we can buffer overflow this

nothing special

item.title: Net Sec Challenge
item.description:
Introduction

“Use this challenge to test your mastery of the skills you have acquired in the Network Security module. All the questions in this challenge can be solved using only nmap, telnet, and hydra.”
cool aparently we can solve it only using those tools , lets try this way

Challenge Questions

Q1 : What is the highest port number being open less than 10,000?
just run
nmap -p 0-10000 $IP
-p mean betwen the port 0 and 10000

item.title: Attacktive Directory
item.description:
Setup

just follow the packets instalation guide
note : for the pip3 install -r /opt/impacketrequiments.txt i used
python3 -m venv .venv source .venv/bin/activate python3 -m pip install -r requirements.txt
because otherwise would’t work , i found out that on stackoverflow , link: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/75602063/pip-install-r-requirements-txt-is-failing-this-environment-is-externally-mana

task 3 welcome to attacktive directory

i runned an nmap scan with a lot of flag , quick description
-A for os detection
-p- to scan port 1 to 65535
-T5 for aggresive template (read more in the man page)

-Pn for pingless way
note thsi scan will take a while

item.title: Farewell
item.description:
Reconing

Lets start with the basiscs to see what ports are open
lets scna the ports with nmap and sS flag for
-sS means Half-Open scan , is a scan quite fast and silent scan which scans for TCP SYN
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Transmission_Control_Protocol
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/SYN


very basiscs ports open , ssh and https , lets check the site


nothing interesting here just a basic login page

apart from that we can find an interesting banner at the top of the site with some active users , maybe we can try some of the mfor the username
